sources.title: NIST Guide to the SI, Chapter 7: Rules and Style Conventions for Expressing Values of Quantities
creators: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sources.version: Web presentation modified 2025-08-18; based on NIST Special Publication 811
sources.locator: Sections 7.1, 7.2, 7.11, and 7.14 on quantity values, unit symbols, quantity equations, numerical-value equations, and quantity dimension
sources.license: NIST public information; facts-only use under NIST Copyrights & Disclaimers

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: The lesson uses factual quantity-and-unit conventions and independently creates all explanations, examples, chains, prompts, and assessments. No NIST wording, table, image, or teaching sequence is reproduced or adapted.

agentAccess.notes: The NIST copyright/disclaimer, technical-series licensing, and robots routes were checked before substantive access. The public page required no login, paywall, access-control bypass, or bulk retrieval.

sources.title: International Vocabulary of Metrology — Basic and General Concepts and Associated Terms (VIM), 3rd edition
creators: Joint Committee for Guides in Metrology
sources.publisher: Bureau International des Poids et Mesures
sources.locator: Entries 1.2 (Note 2), 1.7 (definition and Note 4), 1.21, and 1.22 on quantity kind, quantity dimension, quantity calculus, and quantity equations
sources.license: JCGM 2012; all rights reserved; reproduction requires permission

uses.rightsBasisEvidence: The lesson uses metrological definitions and relations as factual authority, while independently creating its explanations, examples, prompts, and assessments. No JCGM wording, image, table, or sequence is reproduced or adapted.

agentAccess.notes: The public JCGM/BIPM definitions, publication record, and BIPM terms were checked. No login, paywall, access-control bypass, or source-specific agent-processing prohibition was found; access was limited to public pages.
